title: Patterns on the Multiplication Table
essential_question: What patterns can you find on a multiplication table?
strategies: Rows and columns show skip counting

items.question: On a multiplication table, what is the product where row 3 and column 4 meet?
Is the product of 2 × 7 even or odd?
What pattern do you see in the 5s column?
The diagonal of a multiplication table shows: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36... What are these called?

items.title: The Multiplication Table
items.content: A multiplication table shows products of all single-digit numbers. Row 3 shows: 3,6,9,12,15,18,21,24,27,30. Column 3 shows the same numbers!

items.title: Even and Odd Patterns
items.content: Even × even = even. Even × odd = even. Odd × odd = odd. If at least one factor is even, the product is even.

items.title: Square Numbers on the Diagonal
items.content: Going diagonally: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100. These are square numbers (a number times itself).

items.question: On the multiplication table, row 4 column 6 = ?
All products in the 2s row are:
items.explanation: 2 is even, so all products are even.

items.question: Row 5 of the table: 5, 10, 15, 20, ?. What comes next?
On the table, row 3 and column 8 give the same product as row 8 column 3. Why?
The 9s column has a pattern in digits: 9,18,27,36,45,54,63,72,81. The tens digit goes up by 1 and the ones digit goes down by 1. What do the two digits always add up to?
How many products on a 1–10 multiplication table are odd?
items.explanation: Odd × odd = odd. There are 5 odd numbers (1,3,5,7,9). 5×5=25 odd products.
